example.japanese: 「而して」は日本語の言葉です。

name: 此の
explanation: A JLPT N5 pre-noun adjectival (rentaishi) meaning "this".

example.japanese: 此の状況を利用すべきだ。
example.english: We have to take advantage of this situation.

name: 余り
explanation: A JLPT N5 noun, no adjective meaning "remainder".

example.japanese: これはお昼の余りです。
example.english: Those are the leftovers from lunch.

name: 一寸
explanation: A JLPT N5 adverb meaning "a little".

example.japanese: 一寸の虫にも五分の魂。

name: 意味
explanation: A JLPT N5 noun, suru noun, transitive verb meaning "meaning".

example.japanese: こんにちは、如何ですか？

name: 全部
explanation: A JLPT N5 noun, no adjective, adverb meaning "all".

name: 其れから
explanation: A JLPT N5 expression meaning "and then".

example.japanese: 「其れから」は日本語の言葉です。
「其れでは」は日本語の言葉です。

name: ゆっくり
explanation: A JLPT N5 adverb, adverb taking the 'to' particle, suru noun meaning "slowly".

example.japanese: 「然うして」は日本語の言葉です。
この件を直ぐに調べてくれ。
example.english: Look into this matter right away.

name: 何の
explanation: A JLPT N5 pre-noun adjectival (rentaishi) meaning "which".

name: もう一度
explanation: A JLPT N5 expression, adverb meaning "once more".

name: こんな
explanation: A JLPT N5 pre-noun adjectival (rentaishi) meaning "this sort of".

name: 其の
explanation: A JLPT N5 pre-noun adjectival (rentaishi) meaning "that".

example.japanese: 其の仕事は正午前に終えねばならない。
example.english: The work must be finished before noon.

example.japanese: 「如何して」は日本語の言葉です。
何故ならば、私は中国語が話せます。
example.english: The reason is because I can speak Chinese.

name: 始め
explanation: A JLPT N5 noun, adverb meaning "beginning".

name: どうも
explanation: A JLPT N5 interjection meaning "thank you".

example.japanese: 彼女はさらにもっと美しい。
未だにあなたのことが大好きです。

name: だんだん
explanation: A JLPT N5 interjection meaning "thank you".

example.japanese: だんだん暖かくなります。
example.english: It will get warmer and warmer.
